title: Create a podman-troubleshooting man page
body:
Generated at build time from troubleshooting.md. Purpose is
to ship an actual man page to end users.

Much more complicated than initial guess, because there was
a bug in my Makefile man page filtering, the sed expression
that cleans up markdown that does not translate to roff.
All I've done here is reorder some of the expressions,
stripping off https links *before* we process
podman man page links.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
Distribute podman-troubleshooting(7) man page
```

comments.body:
podman-troubleshooting troubleshoots issues in

podman run, build, push, commit, pull, pod create, images, machine
podman-remote

> podman-troubleshooting troubleshoots issues in
> 
> podman run, build, push, commit, pull, pod create, images, machine podman-remote

Meaning, for each of those man pages, I should add a link to podman-troubleshooting? If so, only in `SEE ALSO`? Or, new `Troubleshooting` section?

Hoist by my own petard -- my man page xref script:
```
inconsistent link podman-troubleshooting(7) -> (URL), expected podman-troubleshooting.7.md
```
...and there's just no clean way to fix that. Will look at it tomorrow.

I think troubleshoot section, so it is more prevalent.
Thanks @edsantiago for doing this, I think it will be helpful.
@rhatdan how's this? I moved `### Troubleshooting` into a subsection of `## SEE ALSO`
